prestazioni: Engine swap to a Lamborghini L539 5.2L V10 (from the Huracán) with a custom dry-sump system by H&R Performance, retaining the original LM003’s 4WD drivetrain but upgrading to a Quaife ATB helical LSD for improved torque distribution

Precision Turbo & Engine PT8285X turbocharger with a custom 4.0-inch V-band downpipe, paired with a Tial MV-R 44mm external wastegate and a custom air-to-water intercooler setup by CSF Racing, utilizing a Bosch Motorsport 0261002047 high-flow fuel pump and ID1000x injectors for E85 compatibility

Ohlins TTX46 coilovers with 3-way adjustable damping, custom valved for the LM003’s tubular spaceframe chassis, paired with Eibach ERS 1000N/mm helper springs and spherical bearing upper mounts by Cusco for zero compliance
estetica: Full carbon fiber widebody conversion by Novitec Torado, extending the LM003’s original clamshell fenders by 3.5 inches per side with integrated NACA ducts for brake cooling, finished in a custom satin-forged weave by Forged Composites

Custom front splitter and rear diffuser by RWB Japan, designed specifically for the LM003’s 1980s rally proportions, with integrated LED light pods by PIAA and a rear wing mount system using titanium bolts from TiFast

Retro-inspired livery by Vellano Designs, featuring a period-correct Martini Racing stripe kit in matte vinyl with hand-painted LM003 script on the hood, paired with HRE FF01 wheels in a 18x11 front / 18x12 rear staggered fitment, wrapped in Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 R tires (285/650R18 front, 305/680R18 rear)
